note_data.title: Industrial Development in Pakistan
keyPoints: At independence in 1947, Pakistan inherited only 34 industrial units out of 921 in undivided India
Industrial development in Pakistan can be divided into four phases: 1947-58, 1958-69, 1969-77, and 1977 onwards
First Five Year Plan (1955-60) emphasized industrial development with focus on consumer goods
Green Revolution in 1960s led to development of agricultural machinery and fertilizer industries
Nationalization policy of 1972 under Bhutto government brought major industries under state control
Denationalization and privatization policy started in 1977 to encourage private sector investment
Major industrial cities include Karachi, Lahore, Faisalabad, Hyderabad, Gujranwala, and Sialkot
Cottage industries like handicrafts, carpets, and sports goods contribute significantly to exports
Pakistan's main industries include textiles, sugar, cement, fertilizer, chemicals, and engineering goods

definitions.definition: The manufacturing of goods in factories by using machines, tools and labor
Small scale manufacturing activity carried out at home with the help of family members using simple tools
Industries that produce machinery, equipment and basic materials like iron, steel and chemicals
Industries that produce consumer goods like textiles, food products and household items
The process of bringing privately owned industries under government control and ownership

importantQuestions.question: Write any four problems of industrial development in Pakistan.
importantQuestions.answer: 1) Lack of capital and investment 2) Shortage of skilled labor 3) Inadequate infrastructure facilities 4) Limited availability of raw materials

importantQuestions.question: Describe the industrial development in Pakistan from 1947 to 1958.
importantQuestions.answer: After independence, Pakistan faced severe industrial challenges as it inherited only 34 industrial units. The government adopted import substitution policy to reduce dependence on imports. Focus was on establishing consumer goods industries like textiles, sugar, and cement. Pakistan Industrial Development Corporation (PIDC) was established in 1950 to promote industrial growth. Private sector was encouraged through various incentives. By 1958, significant progress was made in textile, jute, paper, and chemical industries. However, lack of heavy industries and dependence on imported machinery remained major problems.

mcqs.question: Pakistan Industrial Development Corporation (PIDC) was established in:
Which industry contributes the highest percentage to Pakistan's exports?
Nationalization of industries was carried out during the government of:

commonMistakes: Students often confuse the dates of Five Year Plans and their industrial focus
Mixing up nationalization (1972) with denationalization (1977) policies
Forgetting to mention PIDC establishment date (1950) in industrial development answers
Not distinguishing between cottage industries and small-scale industries
Writing about Indian industrial cities instead of Pakistani ones like Karachi, Lahore, Faisalabad
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs about PIDC, nationalization dates, and major industries
Always mention specific years: 1950 for PIDC, 1972 for nationalization, 1977 for privatization
Learn the four phases of industrial development with their time periods
Memorize major industrial cities and their main industries for short questions
Problems and solutions of industrial development is a favorite long question topic
note_data.keyDiagram: Map of Pakistan showing major industrial cities and their main industries - Karachi (textiles, chemicals), Lahore (textiles, sports goods), Faisalabad (textiles), Hyderabad (glass, matches), Gujranwala (electrical goods), Sialkot (sports goods, surgical instruments)
